name: Search existing consignee
caption: Traders
heading: Search for an existing consignee
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (search panel and results); search inputs use govuk-grid-column-one-third

structure.govukComponent: visually-hidden caption (govuk-visually-hidden) for the results table

structure.text: Columns: Name | Address | Country | View | Select. One row per matching trader; View and Select are per-row buttons.
Per-result stacked cards (H3 Name / Address / Country + View + Select) shown at narrow widths
structure.govukComponent: custom transporter-table-mobile (non-standard responsive variant of the table)

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Search for an existing consignee'
Groups the Name and Address search inputs under a legend 'Search'
Name and Address free-text search fields; labels are plain govuk-label (no size modifier) and inputs are plain govuk-input (no width/error modifiers)
Search results — columns Name, Address, Country, View, Select
'Create a new consignee' — escape hatch to the create-new consignee flow (id add-economic-operator)
Visible divider between search panel and results

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: button.link-button (View / Select buttons in each results row)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Per-result-row action buttons styled to look like links; 'View' opens the trader detail, 'Select' picks that existing consignee for the notification.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Buttons visually restyled as links — not a standard Design System pattern. Each row is its own mini-form (multiple crumb/etag hidden inputs observed), so View/Select are POST submits, not links.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Table with an actions column using govuk-link (for View) and a govuk-button or govuk-button--secondary (for Select), or a plain link column.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 99 (structure eval — repeated BUTTON.link-button View/Select)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: custom pagination widget — classes pagination, pagination-list, pagination-item, pagination-item-next, pagination-link, pagination-link-icon, pagination-link-label, pagination-link-title
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke pagination control rendering 'Next page : 2 of 4000' with an icon and label.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Entirely custom, not the GOV.UK Pagination component. Note '4000' pages of results implies an unfiltered/very large address book — the search must actually narrow results.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 99 (non-govuk class inventory + before snapshot Pagination nav)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Dual rendering of the same results — a desktop table and a mobile stacked-card layout (H3 Name/Address/Country per card) toggled by CSS, each wrapped in its own form.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke responsive-table implementation with duplicated desktop/mobile markup and duplicate forms. Adds markup weight and two code paths for one dataset.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single GOV.UK Table (which is responsive by default) or the responsive-table pattern from the Design System, rendered once.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific wrapper/id classes around the search panel and per-column result content.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: App-specific hooks for styling/JS; harmless but not portable. The 'economic-operator' naming reveals the internal domain term for consignee (economic operator).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-form-group / govuk-grid-* wrappers; no bespoke classes needed.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.corroboration: No QA test drives this field. Every ched-p, ched-d and ched-a workflow reaches the consignee flow via the 'Create a new consignee' escape hatch and never fills a search term (ched-p-workflows.ts:404, :753, :959; ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:156). That the ConsigneeCreation page is reachable without ever searching corroborates required=false at the page level, but the field's filter semantics remain a trace-only lower bound.

fields.name: address
fields.label: Address
fields.corroboration: As with 'name': no QA test fills this field; the search step is always bypassed (ched-p-workflows.ts:404). Optional/skippable confirmed, filter behaviour unobserved by both traces and tests.

fields.name: results-table-row
fields.label: Traders search results table
fields.hint: Not a form input — a results list rendered as a table with per-row action buttons (NOT radio inputs). 'Select' chooses that existing consignee; 'View' shows its detail. The only inputs in the results markup are hidden crumb/etag CSRF tokens; there is no radio/checkbox selection control.
observedValues: Linus George Ltd — 558 Oak Street, Ut aperiam in volupt, Nisi tempore aliqua, Eligendi et beatae p, 24271 — Switzerland
Astra Rosales — 42 East Hague Extension, Delectus sit odio p, Laborum Odio tempor, Quas occaecat ut ear, 30055 — Switzerland
Astra Rosales — 43 East Hague Extension, ... — England
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 99 (before snapshot table e78; structure eval)
fields.corroboration: No QA test ever clicks a 'Select' or 'View' row button. The select-an-existing-consignee path is entirely untested — the automation always creates a fresh consignee instead. The only locator the page object exposes for this page is linkCreateNewConsignee (SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts:6-8). The results table and its Select/View actions are a pure trace-confirmed lower bound with zero test corroboration.

fields.name: create-new-consignee-link
fields.label: Create a new consignee
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 99 (clicked; id add-economic-operator, routes to /traders/consignee/new)
fields.corroboration: Corroborated exactly by the page object: SearchExistingConsigneePage.linkCreateNewConsignee = getByRole('link', { name: 'Create a new consignee' }) (SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts:6-8). This is the ONLY control on this page any QA test interacts with, and it is the sole route the automation uses off this page (ched-p-workflows.ts:404). Sibling pages follow the identical pattern — 'Create a new consignor or exporter' (SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts:6-8) and 'Create a new transporter' (SearchExistingTransporterPage.ts:6-8) — confirming this is shared address-book chrome, not a consignee-specific design.

validationMessages.message: No validation error observed on this page in this trace
validationMessages.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 99 — .govuk-error-summary absent; search fields are optional (required=false). Behaviour on empty search unknown. No QA test asserts any validation message for this page (the search step is always bypassed), so there is no inferred error copy either — genuine gap.

requirements.requirement: The Search Existing Consignee page must pass automated accessibility (axe) checks.
requirements.evidence: ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:155 (checkPageAccessibility(pages.page, 'Search Existing Consignee Page')); same assertion in ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:116. This is a human-stated rule that the page renders accessibly.

openQuestions: QA automation NEVER exercises the search-and-select path: every ched-p/ched-d/ched-a workflow and both accessibility specs reach the consignee via the 'Create a new consignee' escape hatch (ched-p-workflows.ts:404, :753, :959). The Name/Address search inputs, the results table, and the per-row Select/View buttons are therefore trace-confirmed rendered reality but have ZERO test corroboration — their filter, selection and validation behaviour is a lower bound only. For the new IUU app this is the key finding: decide whether 'select an existing consignee' is a required capability at all, or whether create-new is the primary/only path.

URL confirmed as /traders/consignee/search (page=1) with the primary-page prompt saying /consignee/search — but the 'Create a new consignee' link routes to /traders/consignee/new. The provided url-pattern slug guessed /consignee/search; actual observed path is /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedRef}/traders/consignee/search.

The consignee search is over the whole address book (pagination reads '2 of 4000') — the new app needs a real search that narrows, not a paginated dump. Confirm whether Name/Address are AND-combined and whether an empty search is allowed.
Both Name and Address inputs are optional (required=false). Behaviour and any validation message when both are blank is unobserved — gap.
Country appears only as a results column, not a search field — confirm there is no country filter on this page.
Internal domain term for a consignee is 'economic operator' (ids economic-operator-name/address, add-economic-operator) — relevant to the new app's data model naming.

This IUU journey is filed by IPAFFS as CHED-P (trace title: 'Submits Valid CHEDP Fish Notification'); the consignee-search page is shared CHED chrome, so it is a generic traders/address-book page rather than IUU-specific.
